Name: Oddish
Flavor-Text: If exposed to moonlight, it starts to move. It roams far and wide at night to scatter its seeds.

Name: Gloom
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its pistils exude an incredibly foul odor. The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.

Name: Vileplume
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any Supporter cards from their hand. If tails, during your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any Item cards from their hand.
Flavor-Text: It has the world’s largest petals. With every step, the petals shake out heavy clouds of toxic pollen.

Name: Paras
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The mushrooms, known as tochukaso, are controlling the bug. Even if the bug bugs the mushrooms, they tell it to bug off.

Name: Parasect
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may make both Active Pokémon Asleep and Poisoned.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The bug is mostly dead, with the mushroom on its back having become the main body. If the mushroom comes off, the bug stops moving.

Name: Wurmple
Effects: Search your deck for any number of Wurmple, Silcoon, Beautifly, Cascoon, and Dustox, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It lives among the tall grass and in forests. It repels attacks by raising up the spikes on its rear.

Name: Silcoon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It drinks dew that collects on its silk and waits for evolution. Its hard cocoon repels attacks.

Name: Beautifly
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has an aggressive nature. It stabs prey with its long, narrow mouth to drain the prey’s fluids.

Name: Cascoon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks if that damage is 60 or less.
Flavor-Text: It never forgets any attack it endured while in the cocoon. After evolution, it seeks payback.

Name: Dustox
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 8 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.
Flavor-Text: Tends to be drawn to bonfires on dark nights. Difficult to chase away from settlements because of the way it scatters highly toxic scales.

Name: Seedot
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If it remains still, it looks just like a real nut. It delights in surprising foraging Pokémon.

Name: Nuzleaf
Effects: You may have your opponent switch their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lives deep in forests. With the leaf on its head, it makes a flute whose song makes listeners uneasy.

Name: Shiftry
Effects: You may have your opponent switch their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Put this Pokémon and all attached cards in the Lost Zone.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that was feared as a forest guardian. It can read the foe’s mind and take preemptive action.

Name: Roselia
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Its flowers give off a relaxing fragrance. The stronger its aroma, the healthier the Roselia is.

Name: Roserade
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack also does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: After captivating opponents with its sweet scent, it lashes them with its thorny whips.

Name: Phantump
Flavor-Text: After a lost child perished in the forest, their spirit possessed a tree stump, causing the spirit’s rebirth as this Pokémon.

Name: Trevenant
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon V, your opponent can’t take any Prize cards for it.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: People fear it due to a belief that it devours any who try to cut down trees in its forest, but to the Pokémon it shares its woods with, it’s kind.

Name: Blipbug
Flavor-Text: A constant collector of information, this Pokémon is very smart. Very strong is what it isn’t.

Name: Dottler
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It barely moves, but it’s still alive. Hiding in its shell without food or water seems to have awakened its psychic powers.

Name: Orbeetle
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may attach up to 3 Energy cards from your opponent’s discard pile to your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s famous for its high level of intelligence, and the large size of its brain is proof that it also possesses immense psychic power.

Name: Slugma
Effects: Attach a [R] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body is made of magma. If it doesn’t keep moving, its body will cool and harden.

Name: Magcargo
Effects: Put all Energy attached to this Pokémon in the Lost Zone.
Flavor-Text: Its body is as hot as lava and is always billowing. Flames will occasionally burst from its shell.

Name: Torkoal
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It burns coal inside its shell for energy. It blows out black soot if it is endangered.

Name: Litwick
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: The flame on its head keeps its body slightly warm. This Pokémon takes lost children by the hand to guide them to the spirit world.

Name: Lampent
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon appears just before someone passes away, so it’s feared as an emissary of death.

Name: Chandelure
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may discard the top 3 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon haunts dilapidated mansions. It sways its arms to hypnotize opponents with the ominous dancing of its flames.

Name: Delphox V
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.
Put 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon in the Lost Zone. This attack also does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Litleo
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: This hot-blooded Pokémon is filled with curiosity. When it gets angry or starts fighting, its short mane gets hot.

Name: Pyroar
Ability-Effect: During Pokémon Checkup, put 4 damage counters on your opponent’s Burned Pokémon instead of 2.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: The temperature of its breath is over 10,000 degrees Fahrenheit, but Pyroar doesn’t use it on its prey. This Pokémon prefers to eat raw meat.

Name: Poliwag
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: For Poliwag, swimming is easier than walking. The swirl pattern on its belly is actually part of the Pokémon’s innards showing through the skin.

Name: Poliwhirl
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Staring at the swirl on its belly causes drowsiness. This trait of Poliwhirl’s has been used in place of lullabies to get children to go to sleep.

Name: Politoed
Effects: If Poliwag is on your Bench, this attack does 60 more damage. If Poliwhirl is on your Bench, this attack does 90 more damage. If Poliwrath is on your Bench, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: At nightfall, these Pokémon appear on the shores of lakes. They announce their territorial claims by letting out cries that sound like shouting.

Name: Seel
Flavor-Text: Thanks to its thick fat, cold seas don’t bother it at all, but it gets tired pretty easily in warm waters.

Name: Dewgong
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Shuffle any amount of [W] Energy from your Pokémon into your deck. This attack does 40 damage for each card you shuffled into your deck in this way.
Flavor-Text: It sunbathes on the beach after meals. The rise in its body temperature helps its digestion.

Name: Horsea
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Horsea makes its home in oceans with gentle currents. If this Pokémon is under attack, it spits out pitch-black ink and escapes.

Name: Seadra
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It’s the males that raise the offspring. While Seadra are raising young, the spines on their backs secrete thicker and stronger poison.

Name: Kingdra
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may have either player shuffle their hand and put it on the bottom of their deck. If that player put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, they draw 4 cards.
Flavor-Text: With the arrival of a storm at sea, this Pokémon will show itself on the surface. When a Kingdra and a Dragonite meet, a fierce battle ensues.

Name: Luvdisc
Effects: Each player reveals the top 2 cards of their deck, then draws those cards.
Flavor-Text: Luvdisc makes its home in coral reefs in warm seas. It especially likes sleeping in the space between Corsola’s branches.

Name: Shellos
Flavor-Text: There’s speculation that its appearance is determined by what it eats, but the truth remains elusive.

Name: Finneon
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a [W] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon that has the Swim Freely attack.
Flavor-Text: It lures in prey with its shining tail fins. It stays near the surface during the day and moves to the depths when night falls.

Name: Lumineon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They traverse the deep waters as if crawling over the seafloor. The fantastic lights of its fins shine like stars in the night sky.

Name: Snover
Flavor-Text: One is likely to encounter this Pokémon while out in the snow. There are stories of Snover appearing in human settlements but doing no harm—rather, they bond with the children.

Name: Abomasnow
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: If it sees any packs of Darumaka going after Snover, it chases them off, swinging its sizable arms like hammers.

Name: Hisuian Basculin
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Though it differs from other Basculin in several respects, including demeanor—this one is gentle—I have categorized it as a regional form given the vast array of shared qualities.

Name: Hisuian Basculegion
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each basic Energy card in your discard pile. Then, shuffle those cards into your deck.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Clads itself in the souls of comrades that perished before fulfilling their goals of journeying upstream. No other species throughout all Hisui’s rivers is Basculegion’s equal.

Name: Ducklett
Flavor-Text: They are better at swimming than flying, and they happily eat their favorite food, peat moss, as they dive underwater.

Name: Swanna
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.

Name: Kyurem V
Effects: Attach any number of [W] Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Kyurem VMAX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may discard the top card of your deck. If that card is a [W] Energy card, attach it to 1 of your Pokémon.
Effects: You may discard any amount of [W] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 50 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Cramorant
Ability-Effect: If you have 4 or more cards in the Lost Zone, ignore all Energy in this Pokémon’s attack costs.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness.
Flavor-Text: It’s so strong that it can knock out some opponents in a single hit, but it also may forget what it’s battling midfight.

Name: Glastrier
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Glastrier emits intense cold from its hooves. It’s also a belligerent Pokémon—anything it wants, it takes by force.

Name: Pikachu
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.

Name: Raichu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
If you have used your VSTAR Power, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high-voltage power.

Name: Electrike
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. It gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

Name: Manectric
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It stimulates its own muscles with electricity, so it can move quickly. It eases its soreness with electricity, too, so it can recover quickly as well.

Name: Magnezone V
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to the new Active Pokémon.
This attack also does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Magnezone VSTAR
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Item cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 90 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rotom V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw 3 cards. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.
Effects: Put any number of Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile in the Lost Zone. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you put in the Lost Zone in this way.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tynamo
Effects: Search your deck for a [L] Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: One alone can emit only a trickle of electricity, so a group of them gathers to unleash a powerful electric shock.

Name: Eelektrik
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.

Name: Eelektross
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s attacks do 120 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

Name: Clefairy
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, for each of your Benched Clefairy, you may search your deck for a [P] Energy card and attach it to that Clefairy. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each [P] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can be found in quiet mountain areas on a full moon’s night. Its dancing and its tiny, faintly glowing wings confer upon it a lovely fairylike quality.

Name: Clefable
Ability-Effect: All of your Pokémon take 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s [N] Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). You can’t apply more than 1 Spirit Charm Ability at a time.
Flavor-Text: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

Name: Gastly
Effects: Put 1 damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Born from gases, anyone would faint if engulfed by its gaseous body, which contains poison.

Name: Haunter
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: This frightful, malevolent spirit can glide through walls, appearing wherever it likes. According to rumor, victims of a Haunter’s lick will wither to death day by day.

Name: Gengar
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may put it onto your Bench. If you do, put 3 damage counters on this Pokémon.
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: On the night of a full moon, if shadows move on their own and laugh, it must be Gengar’s doing.

Name: Mr. Mime
Effects: You and your opponent play Rock-Paper-Scissors until someone wins. If you win, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The behavior of this clown-like Pokémon reminds one of pantomime. It creates invisible walls using a force emitted from its fingertips.

Name: Jynx
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its strange cries sound like human language. There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.

Name: Radiant Gardevoir
Ability-Effect: All of your Pokémon take 20 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon V (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has the power to predict the future. Its power peaks when it is protecting its Trainer.

Name: Sableye
Effects: You can use this attack only if you have 10 or more cards in the Lost Zone. Put 12 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is feared. When its gemstone eyes begin to glow with a sinister shine, it’s believed that Sableye will steal people’s spirits away.

Name: Mawile
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat. During your next turn, the Defending Pokémon takes 90 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It uses its docile-looking face to lull foes into complacency, then bites with its huge, relentless jaws.

Name: Shuppet
Flavor-Text: There’s a proverb that says, “Shun the house where Shuppet gather in the growing dusk.”

Name: Banette
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand. If you do, put this Pokémon in the Lost Zone. (Discard all attached cards.)
Flavor-Text: Resentment at being cast off made it spring into being. Some say that treating it well will satisfy it, and it will once more become a stuffed toy.

Name: Cresselia
Effects: Move 2 damage counters from each of your Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Those who sleep holding Cresselia’s feather are assured of joyful dreams. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

Name: Hisuian Zorua
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: A once-departed soul, returned to life in Hisui. Derives power from resentment, which rises as energy atop its head and takes on the forms of foes. In this way, Zorua vents lingering malice.

Name: Hisuian Zoroark
Effects: At the end of your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon will be Knocked Out.
Put a card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: With its disheveled white fur, it looks like an embodiment of death. Heedless of its own safety, Zoroark attacks its nemeses with a bitter energy so intense, it lacerates Zoroark’s own body.

Name: Inkay
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It spins while making its luminescent spots flash. These spots allow it to communicate with others by using different patterns of light.

Name: Malamar
Effects: Each player reveals their hand. If a card in your opponent’s hand has the same name as a card in your hand, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Gazing at its luminescent spots will quickly induce a hypnotic state, putting the observer under Malamar’s control.

Name: Comfey
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Put the other card in the Lost Zone.
Flavor-Text: Comfey picks flowers with its vine and decorates itself with them. For some reason, flowers won’t wither once they’re attached to a Comfey.

Name: Mimikyu
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It wears a rag fashioned into a Pikachu costume in an effort to look less scary. Unfortunately, the costume only makes it creepier.

Name: Spectrier
Effects: Choose 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon and put 2 damage counters on each of them.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Phantom Strike.
Flavor-Text: It probes its surroundings with all its senses save one—it doesn’t use its sense of sight. Spectrier’s kicks are said to separate soul from body.

Name: Enamorus V
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s Pokémon’s Abilities done to each of your Pokémon that has any [P] Energy attached, except any Enamorus V.
Effects: Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hisuian Growlithe
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: They patrol their territory in pairs. I believe the igneous rock components in the fur of this species are the result of volcanic activity in its habitat.

Name: Hisuian Arcanine
Effects: If you have no cards in your hand, this attack does 150 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Snaps at its foes with fangs cloaked in blazing flame. Despite its bulk, it deftly feints every which way, leading opponents on a deceptively merry chase as it all but dances around them.

Name: Poliwrath
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Put 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Its body is solid muscle. When swimming through cold seas, Poliwrath uses its impressive arms to smash through drift ice and plow forward.

Name: Machop
Flavor-Text: Its whole body is composed of muscles. Even though it’s the size of a human child, it can hurl 100 grown-ups.

Name: Machoke
Flavor-Text: A sturdy creature boasting a robust physique and boundless stamina. Loves training above all else and voluntarily assists with tasks such as construction and clearing land.

Name: Machamp
Ability-Effect: If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this Pokémon gets +150 HP.
Effects: You may do 100 more damage. If you do, during your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It quickly swings its four arms to rock its opponents with ceaseless punches and chops from all angles.

Name: Rhyhorn
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Strong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging tackles.

Name: Rhydon
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It begins walking on its hind legs after evolution. It can punch holes through boulders with its horn.

Name: Rhyperior
Effects: Discard the top card of your deck. If that card is an Energy card, this attack does 100 more damage, and attach that card to this Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It can load up to three projectiles per arm into the holes in its hands. What launches out of those holes could be either rocks or Roggenrola.

Name: Aerodactyl V
Effects: Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Aerodactyl VSTAR
Effects: Put the top 3 cards of your deck in the Lost Zone.

Until this Pokémon leaves play, it gains an Ability that has the effect “Your opponent’s Pokémon V in play, except any Aerodactyl VSTAR, have no Abilities.” (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Sudowoodo
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: Though it pretends to be a tree, it fails to fool even children. To the touch, its body feels more like rock than tree bark. Sudowoodo’s extreme aversion to water merits special note.

Name: Gligar
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It flies straight at its target’s face, then clamps down on the startled victim to inject poison.

Name: Gliscor
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads. If at least 2 of them are heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its flight is soundless. It uses its lengthy tail to carry off its prey… Then its elongated fangs do the rest.

Name: Makuhita
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: There’s a rumor of a traditional recipe for stew that Trainers can use to raise strong Makuhita.

Name: Hariyama
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Hariyama that are big and fat aren’t necessarily strong. There are some small ones that move nimbly and use moves skillfully.

Name: Meditite
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It eats just one berry a day. By enduring hunger, its spirit is tempered and made sharper.

Name: Medicham
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [F] Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe’s next move.

Name: Relicanth
Effects: Put up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Rock-hard scales and oil-filled swim bladders allow this Pokémon to survive the intense water pressure of the deep sea.

Name: Gastrodon
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon and heal all damage from it.
This attack also does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its search for food sometimes leads it onto land, where it leaves behind a sticky trail of slime as it passes through.

Name: Mienfoo
Flavor-Text: In one minute, a well-trained Mienfoo can chop with its arms more than 100 times.

Name: Mienshao
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: When Mienshao comes across a truly challenging opponent, it will lighten itself by biting off the fur on its arms.

Name: Landorus
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: From the forces of lightning and wind, it creates energy to give nutrients to the soil and make the land abundant.

Name: Binacle
Flavor-Text: After two Binacle find a suitably sized rock, they adhere themselves to it and live together. They cooperate to gather food during high tide.

Name: Barbaracle
Ability-Effect: Your opponent puts any Prize cards they would take in the Lost Zone instead of into their hand.
Flavor-Text: Seven Binacle come together to form one Barbaracle. The Binacle that serves as the head gives orders to those serving as the limbs.

Name: Carbink
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Item cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: When beset by attackers, Carbink wipes them all out by firing high-energy beams from the gems embedded in its body.

Name: Rockruff
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can bond very strongly with its Trainer, but it also has a habit of biting. Raising a Rockruff for a long time can be challenging.

Name: Falinks
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness. If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Five of them are troopers, and one is the brass. The brass’s orders are absolute.

Name: Stonjourner
Effects: Attach up to 2 [F] Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Put the top card of your opponent’s deck in the Lost Zone.
Flavor-Text: It stands in grasslands, watching the sun’s descent from zenith to horizon. This Pokémon has a talent for delivering dynamic kicks.

Name: Spinarak
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Although the poison from its fangs isn’t that strong, it’s potent enough to weaken prey that gets caught in its web.

Name: Ariados
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It spews threads from its mouth to catch its prey. When night falls, it leaves its web to go hunt aggressively.

Name: Murkrow
Flavor-Text: It searches for shiny things for its boss. Murkrow’s presence is said to be unlucky, so many people detest it.

Name: Honchkrow
Effects: Move all Energy from this Pokémon to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: Its goons take care of most of the fighting for it. The only time it dirties its own hands is in delivering a final blow to finish off an opponent.

Name: Seviper
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched [D] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Constant polishing makes the edge of the blade on its tail extremely sharp. It’s Zangoose’s archrival.

Name: Spiritomb
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: This attack does 60 more damage for each Spiritomb in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: Exactly 108 spirits gathered to become this Pokémon. Apparently there are some ill-natured spirits in the mix.

Name: Drapion V
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon’s attacks cost [C] less for each of your opponent’s Single Strike, Rapid Strike, and Fusion Strike Pokémon in play.
Effects: This attack also does 60 damage to 1 of your Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Drapion VSTAR
Ability-Effect: During your turn, you may make your opponent’s Active Pokémon Paralyzed and Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 3 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
Effects: This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Darkrai
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: On a moonless night, a strange incident occurred in which every one of a village’s inhabitants suffered nightmares. The villagers attested that Darkrai appeared before them in these nightmares.

Name: Inkay
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It spins while making its luminescent spots flash. These spots allow it to communicate with others by using different patterns of light.

Name: Hoopa
Effects: Search your deck for a [D] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It gathers things it likes and passes them through its loop to teleport them to a secret place.

Name: Radiant Hisuian Sneasler
Ability-Effect: During Pokémon Checkup, put 2 more damage counters on your opponent’s Poisoned Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Because of Sneasler’s virulent poison and daunting physical prowess, no other species could hope to best it on the frozen highlands. Preferring solitude, this species does not form packs.

Name: Radiant Steelix
Effects: Attach up to 2 [M] Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Discard cards from the top of your deck until only 1 card remains. This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: It is said that if an Onix lives for over 100 years, its composition changes to become diamond-like.

Name: Bronzor
Flavor-Text: It appears in ancient ruins. The pattern on its body doesn’t come from any culture in the Galar region, so it remains shrouded in mystery.

Name: Bronzong
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: Some believe that its bell-like cry opens holes to another world. It has been revered as a deity since ancient times.

Name: Galarian Stunfisk
Effects: If your opponent has a Stadium in play, discard it. If you discarded a Stadium in this way, discard 2 Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Living in mud with a high iron content has given it a strong steel body.

Name: Magearna
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 60 more damage, and your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Built roughly 500 years ago by a scientist, the part called the Soul-Heart is the actual life-form.

Name: Galarian Perrserker V
Effects: Draw 3 cards.
This attack does 20 damage for each card in your hand.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Giratina V
Effects: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and put 2 of them into your hand. Put the other cards in the Lost Zone.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Giratina VSTAR
Effects: Put 2 Energy attached to your Pokémon in the Lost Zone.
You can use this attack only if you have 10 or more cards in the Lost Zone. Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Knocked Out. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Goomy
Flavor-Text: Goomy hides away in the shade of trees, where it’s nice and humid. If the slime coating its body dries out, the Pokémon instantly becomes lethargic.

Name: Hisuian Sliggoo
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: A creature given to melancholy. I suspect its metallic shell developed as a result of the mucus on its skin reacting with the iron in Hisui’s water.

Name: Hisuian Goodra
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to each of your Basic Pokémon that has any [M] Energy attached by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon V.
Flavor-Text: Able to freely control the hardness of its metallic shell. It loathes solitude and is extremely clingy—it will fume and run riot if those dearest to it ever leave its side.

Name: Hisuian Goodra V
Effects: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hisuian Goodra VSTAR
Ability-Effect: During your turn, you may heal all damage from this Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 80 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pidgeot V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may shuffle it and all attached cards into your deck.
Effects: If you have a Stadium in play, this attack does 80 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Lickitung
Flavor-Text: If this Pokémon’s sticky saliva gets on you and you don’t clean it off, an intense itch will set in. The itch won’t go away, either.

Name: Lickilicky
Flavor-Text: Lickilicky’s strange tongue can stretch to many times the length of its body. No one has figured out how Lickilicky’s tongue can stretch so far.

Name: Porygon
Effects: Look at the top 4 cards of either player’s deck and put them back in any order.
Flavor-Text: State-of-the-art technology was used to create Porygon. It was the first artificial Pokémon to be created via computer programming.

Name: Porygon2
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon Tool card in the Lost Zone (both yours and your opponent’s).
Flavor-Text: This is a Porygon that was updated with special data. Porygon2 develops itself by learning about many different subjects all on its own.

Name: Porygon-Z
Effects: Devolve 1 of your opponent’s evolved Pokémon by removing any number of Evolution cards from it. Your opponent shuffles those cards into their deck.
Flavor-Text: Porygon-Z had a program installed to allow it to move between dimensions, but the program also caused instability in Porygon-Z’s behavior.

Name: Snorlax
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)
Effects: This Pokémon is now Asleep. During Pokémon Checkup, flip 2 coins instead of 1. If either of them is tails, this Pokémon is still Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

Name: Aipom
Effects: Look at the top card of your opponent’s deck. You may have your opponent shuffle their deck.
Flavor-Text: This treetop dweller possesses a tail as dexterous as a hand. Ancient writings describe this Pokémon as a one-armed oddity.

Name: Ambipom
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. Search your deck for a number of cards up to the number of heads and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: It uses its tails for everything. If it wraps both of its tails around you and gives you a squeeze, that’s proof it really likes you.

Name: Hisuian Zoroark V
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hisuian Zoroark VSTAR
Ability-Effect: During your turn, you may discard your hand and draw 7 cards. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each of your Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Bouffalant
Effects: Put an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon in the Lost Zone.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon can crush a car with no more than a headbutt. Bouffalant with more hair on their heads hold higher positions within the herd.

Name: Komala
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is Asleep and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, your opponent can’t take any Prize cards for it.
Effects: This Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It remains asleep from birth to death as a result of the sedative properties of the leaves that form its diet.

Name: Skwovet
Flavor-Text: Found throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon becomes uneasy if its cheeks are ever completely empty of berries.

Name: Greedent
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Discard your hand. If you discarded 5 or more cards in this way, this attack does 150 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It stashes berries in its tail—so many berries that they fall out constantly. But this Pokémon is a bit slow-witted, so it doesn’t notice the loss.

Name: Arc Phone
Description: Look at the top card of your deck. You may switch that card with 1 of your face-down Prize cards. (The cards stay face down.)
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Arezu
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 Evolution Pokémon that don’t have a Rule Box, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Box of Disaster
Description: If the Pokémon V this card is attached to has full HP and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, put 8 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Colress’s Experiment
Description: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and put 3 of them into your hand. Put the other cards in the Lost Zone.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Damage Pump
Description: Move up to 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Fantina
Description:
You can use this card only if you have 10 or more cards in the Lost Zone.
During your opponent’s next turn, all of your Pokémon take 120 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon V (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (This includes Pokémon that come into play during that turn.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Iscan
Description: Draw 2 cards. If your Active Pokémon has “Hisuian” in its name, draw 2 more cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Lady
Description: Search your deck for up to 4 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Lake Acuity
Description: All Pokémon that have any [W] or [F] Energy attached (both yours and your opponent’s) take 20 less damage from attacks from the opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Lost City
Description: Whenever a Pokémon (either yours or your opponent’s) is Knocked Out, put that Pokémon in the Lost Zone instead of the discard pile. (Discard all attached cards.)
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Lost Vacuum
Description:
You can use this card only if you put another card from your hand in the Lost Zone.
Choose a Pokémon Tool attached to any Pokémon, or any Stadium in play, and put it in the Lost Zone.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Mirage Gate
Description:
You can use this card only if you have 7 or more cards in the Lost Zone.
Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards of different types and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Miss Fortune Sisters
Description: Look at the top 5 cards of your opponent’s deck and discard any number of Item cards you find there. Your opponent shuffles the other cards back into their deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Panic Mask
Description: Prevent all damage done to the Pokémon this card is attached to by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon that have 40 HP or less remaining.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Riley
Description: Reveal the top 5 cards of your deck and have your opponent choose 2 of them. Discard the chosen cards and put the remaining cards into your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Thorton
Description: Choose a Basic Pokémon in your discard pile and switch it with 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Tool Box
Description: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal any number of Pokémon Tool cards you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Volo
Description: Discard 1 of your Benched Pokémon V and all attached cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Windup Arm
Description: The Pokémon this card is attached to can attack even if it’s Asleep or Paralyzed.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Gift Energy
Description:
As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [C] Energy.
If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand.

Name: Hisuian Electrode V
Effects: This attack does 100 damage for each Special Condition affecting this Pokémon.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Delphox V
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.
Put 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon in the Lost Zone. This attack also does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Kyurem V
Effects: Attach any number of [W] Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Magnezone V
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to the new Active Pokémon.
This attack also does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rotom V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw 3 cards. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.
Effects: Put any number of Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile in the Lost Zone. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you put in the Lost Zone in this way.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rotom V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw 3 cards. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.
Effects: Put any number of Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile in the Lost Zone. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you put in the Lost Zone in this way.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Enamorus V
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s Pokémon’s Abilities done to each of your Pokémon that has any [P] Energy attached, except any Enamorus V.
Effects: Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Aerodactyl V
Effects: Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Aerodactyl V
Effects: Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gallade V
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card you have taken.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Drapion V
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon’s attacks cost [C] less for each of your opponent’s Single Strike, Rapid Strike, and Fusion Strike Pokémon in play.
Effects: This attack also does 60 damage to 1 of your Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Galarian Perrserker V
Effects: Draw 3 cards.
This attack does 20 damage for each card in your hand.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Galarian Perrserker V
Effects: Draw 3 cards.
This attack does 20 damage for each card in your hand.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and put 2 of them into your hand. Put the other cards in the Lost Zone.

V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: If you have a Stadium in play, this attack does 80 more damage.

Name: Volo
Description: Discard 1 of your Benched Pokémon V and all attached cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Kyurem VMAX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may discard the top card of your deck. If that card is a [W] Energy card, attach it to 1 of your Pokémon.
Effects: You may discard any amount of [W] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 50 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Magnezone VSTAR
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Item cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 90 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Aerodactyl VSTAR
Effects: Put the top 3 cards of your deck in the Lost Zone.

Until this Pokémon leaves play, it gains an Ability that has the effect “Your opponent’s Pokémon V in play, except any Aerodactyl VSTAR, have no Abilities.” (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Drapion VSTAR
Ability-Effect: During your turn, you may make your opponent’s Active Pokémon Paralyzed and Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 3 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
Effects: This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

Name: Hisuian Zoroark VSTAR
Ability-Effect: During your turn, you may discard your hand and draw 7 cards. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each of your Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Box of Disaster
Description: If the Pokémon V this card is attached to has full HP and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, put 8 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Collapsed Stadium
Description: Each player can’t have more than 4 Benched Pokémon. If a player has 5 or more Benched Pokémon, they discard Benched Pokémon until they have 4 Pokémon on the Bench. The player who played this card discards first. If more than one effect changes the number of Benched Pokémon allowed, use the smaller number.
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Dark Patch
Description: Attach a basic [D] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched [D] Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Lost Vacuum
Description:
You can use this card only if you put another card from your hand in the Lost Zone.
Choose a Pokémon Tool attached to any Pokémon, or any Stadium in play, and put it in the Lost Zone.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Parasect
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may make both Active Pokémon Asleep and Poisoned.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Mushroom-lacking specimens of this Pokémon lie unmoving in the forest, lending credence to the hypothesis that the large mushroom is in control of Parasect’s actions.

Name: Roserade
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack also does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: After captivating opponents with its sweet scent, it lashes them with its thorny whips.

Name: Charizard
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 3 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Discard the other cards.
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each Leon card in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: It spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. It may cause forest fires by blowing flames.

Name: Chandelure
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may discard the top 3 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon haunts dilapidated mansions. It sways its arms to hypnotize opponents with the ominous dancing of its flames.

Name: Pikachu
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Possesses cheek sacs in which it stores electricity. This clever forest-dweller roasts tough berries with an electric shock before consuming them.

Name: Gengar
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may put it onto your Bench. If you do, put 3 damage counters on this Pokémon.
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Possesses potential victims’ shadows in an effort to steal away the victims’ lives. If your shadow begins to laugh, you must take hold of a protective charm posthaste!

Name: Banette
Effects: Put up to 7 damage counters on this Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter you placed in this way.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Resentment at being cast off made it spring into being. Some say that treating it well will satisfy it, and it will once more become a stuffed toy.

Name: Hisuian Arcanine
Effects: If you have no cards in your hand, this attack does 150 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Snaps at its foes with fangs cloaked in blazing flame. Despite its bulk, it deftly feints every which way, leading opponents on a deceptively merry chase as it all but dances around them.

Name: Spiritomb
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: This attack does 60 more damage for each Spiritomb in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: It lays curses by thinking wicked thoughts. Writings tell that this Pokémon was born out of the assembly of five score and eight malevolent spirits.

Name: Snorlax
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)
Effects: This Pokémon is now Asleep. During Pokémon Checkup, flip 2 coins instead of 1. If either of them is tails, this Pokémon is still Asleep.
Flavor-Text: This glutton appears in villages without warning and devours the entirety of their rice granaries—such occurrences have long been counted among the gravest of disasters.

Name: Castform
Ability-Effect: If you have 8 or more Stadium cards in your discard pile, ignore all Energy in this Pokémon’s attack costs.
Effects: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Its form changes depending on the weather. The rougher conditions get, the rougher Castform’s disposition!

Name: Orbeetle V
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Orbeetle VMAX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Centiskorch V
Effects: You may discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Centiskorch VMAX
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you did any damage with this attack, you may attach a [R] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Pikachu V
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [L] Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pikachu VMAX
Effects: You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 150 more damage.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Enamorus V
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s Pokémon’s Abilities done to each of your Pokémon that has any [P] Energy attached, except any Enamorus V.
Effects: Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gallade V
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card you have taken.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Crobat V
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. You can’t use more than 1 Dark Asset Ability each turn.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Eternatus V
Effects: You may attach a [D] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon VMAX, this attack does 120 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Eternatus VMAX
Ability-Effect: If all of your Pokémon in play are [D] type, you can have up to 8 Pokémon on your Bench, and you can’t put non-[D] Pokémon into play. (If this Ability stops working, discard Pokémon from your Bench until you have 5.)
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each of your [D] Pokémon in play.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Adventurer’s Discovery
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon V, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Boss’s Orders
Description: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.
Flavor-Text: Lysandre

Name: Cook
Description: Heal 70 damage from your Active Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Kabu
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 4 cards. If your Active Pokémon is your only Pokémon in play, draw 8 cards instead.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Nessa
Description: Put up to 4 in any combination of [W] Pokémon and [W] Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Opal
Description: Flip 2 coins. Search your deck for a number of cards up to the number of heads, put them into your hand, and shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Pikachu VMAX
Effects: You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 150 more damage.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mew VMAX
Effects: Choose 1 of your Benched Fusion Strike Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.
